action: Tag LH/RH assemblies, mark orientation, bag screws/clips/springs, and photograph both faces before teardown.
output_record: Tagged photo set plus bag labels
notes: Use the May 3 photo as intake evidence; add close-ups before removing glass or rubbers.

action: Check glass cracks/chips, tint condition, frame corrosion, lower tabs, pivots, latch hardware, rubber shrinkage, and drain/weep paths.
gate_or_hold: No replacement buy before this inspection is recorded.
output_record: Inspection notes with reusable/replace decision per subpart
notes: Classify glass, metal frame, latch/pivot hardware, and seals separately.

action: Remove loose hardware and rubbers only as needed; protect glass edges and avoid forcing bonded or brittle rubber.
gate_or_hold: Stop if glass or frame starts loading.
output_record: Disassembly photos and parts tray inventory
notes: Do not mix LH/RH screws or shims.

action: Wire-brush small tabs/channels by hand, de-rust, neutralize, epoxy-prime, and topcoat/refinish metal contact surfaces.
gate_or_hold: No paint over active rust or trapped rubber residue.
notes: Keep sealing faces smooth so new or reused rubber can seat.

action: Clean and lubricate pivots/latches/sliders, confirm free movement, and replace only damaged fasteners or springs.
gate_or_hold: No refit if mechanism binds or has missing retention hardware.
notes: Use light lubricant compatible with rubber and painted surfaces.

action: Clean glass, inspect perimeter fit, decide reuse versus replacement seals, and capture seal profile measurements if replacement is needed.
gate_or_hold: Seal buy held until profile and length are measured.
output_record: Seal decision row or procurement update
notes: Prefer exact profile match; avoid generic rubber until trial fit proves compression.

action: Assemble temporarily, check latch closure, frame seating, glass clearance, and run a controlled water test off the vehicle.
gate_or_hold: No WP02 closure until water path is understood.
notes: Record any leak point before final body refit.

action: Store wrapped and tagged with hardware bags attached; release to refit only after WP01 body sealing and paint contact surfaces are closed.
notes: This sub-job feeds WP02 and then WP06 final reassembly.

action: Bring front windshield, side glass, vent/quarter assemblies, rear hatch/window mechanisms, and window rubbers into one windows workstream so repair and seal buys are not split across body/chassis rows.
gate_or_hold: Do not buy a complete seal kit before the actual glass/channel inventory is tagged.
notes: May 17 window photos start the wider intake beyond the earlier front vent assemblies.

action: Tag and inspect latches, pivots, sliders, channels, fasteners, shims, and clips by window location.
notes: Separate mechanism service from rubber sourcing so missing or seized hardware is caught before refit.

action: Measure old rubber/weatherstrip profile, total length, groove width, glass thickness, lip direction, and compression gap for each fixed, sliding, vent, and rear window location.
gate_or_hold: No replacement rubber order without profile and length evidence.
notes: Use the May 17 ruler photos as intake evidence; final release still needs calipers/profile close-ups.

action: Compare reusable seals, supplier samples, and any model-specific kit against the actual glass/channel before buying a full set.
gate_or_hold: Reject generic rubber that has not been trial-fitted.
notes: Prefer one matched seal route where possible; keep old pieces as templates until receipt check passes.

action: After frame repair and seal choice, bench-fit glass into cleaned channels, test latch/slider movement, and run a controlled water check before final body refit.
gate_or_hold: No final body refit with untested windows.
notes: Record leak points and adjustment needs while access is still good.
